llm_response: the fall of the roman empire was a complex process driven by internal and external pressures over several centuries. political instability weakened central authority, allowing regional governors to act independently and eroding the unity of the state. economic decline followed as trade routes collapsed and inflation soared, leaving the population unable to support the vast imperial machinery. military defeats against the huns and other groups, combined with the relentless barbarian invasions, eventually breached the defensive borders of the western province. the resulting barbarian invasions led to the fragmentation of the territory and the rise of new kingdoms in europe.
